title: cytisus hirsutus subsp. ciliatus
intro: Cytisus hirsutus subsp. ciliatus, also known as hairy broom or Scotch broom, is a type of flowering shrub belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cytisus hirsutus subsp. ciliatus include its potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions due to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in tinctures or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
